metadata.title: Cambridge Flyers Gold Mock 1
metadata.description: Manually authored A2 Flyers gold mock — full 59-item form with listening, reading & writing, and speaking tasks aligned to the M2.0 blueprint.
metadata.authoringNotes: M4.1 Gold Mock — manually authored and academically reviewed.

audio.transcript:
Examiner: Look at the pictures. Listen and draw a line from each name to the correct person.

This is Mrs Chen. She's selling flowers at the festival stall.
Omar is playing the drums on the main stage.
Priya is taking photos of the dancers.
Mr Lopez cooks paella at the food stand every year.
Finn's got a bright green festival wristband on his arm.
Zoe is standing next to the painting display near the entrance.
The mayor gave a short speech on the platform last night.
audio.caption: Link names to pictures

audio.transcript:
Examiner: Listen and write the missing word or number.

The guest works as an engineer at a technology company.
The students are building a robot for the science fair.
Tomorrow the temperature will be twenty-two degrees.
The train to Brighton is leaving from platform five.
The old castle was built in eleven fifty.
Maria was the fastest runner in the school competition.
You need two hundred grams of flour for the cake.
audio.caption: Write words or numbers

audio.transcript:
Examiner: Listen and tick the correct picture.

The explorers are carefully crossing a wooden bridge over the river.
From the top of the hill they can see a green valley and a lake.
Last night there was a thunderstorm but everyone stayed safe in the tents.
They cook a tasty vegetable stew on the campfire every evening.
The campsite is next to a forest path, two kilometres from the village.
Tomorrow morning they will canoe on the river if the weather is good.
They thought the eagle was the most interesting animal on the walk.
audio.caption: Tick the correct picture

audio.transcript:
Examiner: Listen and colour or draw.

Colour the shop sign turquoise.
Draw a bicycle next to the bus stop sign.
Colour the woman's scarf scarlet.
Draw an arrow from the boy to the library.
Draw three clouds in the sky above the street.
Colour the post box red.
Draw a cat sitting on the garden wall.
audio.caption: Colour and draw

questions.questionText: Read the sentence. Choose the correct picture.

content.passage: Runners can collect sponsorship money to help build a new children's hospital.

questions.questionText: Read and choose the best answer.

content.passage:
Interview with Dr Reyes — marine biologist

I've always loved the ocean. I studied biology at university and now I work on a research boat. Last summer we discovered a new species of fish near the coral reef. Plastic pollution is a big problem, so our team also teaches schools about protecting the sea.

choices.text: Dr Reyes works on a research boat.
Dr Reyes is a football coach.
The team found plastic in a supermarket.

questions.questionText: Read and choose the best answer.

content.passage: The Old Observatory was built in 1892 on a hill above the town. Astronomers used it to study the stars before modern computers existed. During the war it closed, but volunteers reopened it as a museum in 1985. Today visitors can look through the original telescope on clear nights.

choices.text: The observatory is now a museum.

questions.questionText: Read and choose the best answer.

content.passage:
Day 3 — Cycling in the countryside

We are cycling along quiet country lanes and stopping at small farms to buy cheese and bread. The hills are tiring but the views are amazing. Tonight we are staying in a guest house where the owner is cooking a traditional meal for all the guests.

choices.text: They are staying in a guest house tonight.
They never stop to buy food.

questions.questionText: Read and choose the best answer.

content.passage:
Green School News

Students must turn off lights when they leave a room. We mustn't leave taps running because water is precious. The eco club planted fifty trees behind the sports field and built bird boxes for the garden. Next term we are going to start a compost project.

choices.text: The eco club planted trees at school.
No birds live near the school.

questions.questionText: Read and choose the best answer.

content.passage: When the path suddenly disappeared in the fog, Maya felt nervous. She was checking her map while her brother was calling the mountain rescue on his phone. They were wearing warm jackets because the temperature was falling quickly. An hour later a guide arrived and led them safely back to the village.

choices.text: A guide helped them return to the village.
They continued alone in the fog.

questions.questionText: Read and choose the best answer.

content.passage: Wearable fitness trackers can count your steps and measure your heart rate. Some models are more accurate than others, especially when you are running. Doctors say trackers are useful, but sleeping well and eating healthy food are more important for long-term health.

choices.text: Some trackers are more accurate than others.
Trackers only work when you swim.

questions.questionText: Read and choose the best answer.

content.passage: The Riverside Arts Festival was the most exciting event of the year. The jazz concert was the loudest performance, but the puppet show was the funniest for younger children. Food stalls sold dishes from twelve countries. Many people said the fireworks at the end were the most beautiful they had ever seen.

choices.text: People enjoyed the fireworks at the end.
There was no music at the festival.

questions.questionText: Look at the two pictures. Tell your partner about the differences.

content.prompt: Look at the two pictures of a city street. Tell your partner about five differences between them.
followUpQuestions: How many cars are in picture A?
What is different about the café?
Is there a cyclist in both pictures?
Where is the bus in picture B?
What colour is the traffic light in picture A?

questions.questionText: Answer the examiner's questions about you.

content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about yourself, your school, hobbies and future plans.
followUpQuestions: Tell me about your best friend.
What are you learning at school this year?
Describe a place you visited recently.
What would you like to do when you are older?
How do you usually spend your weekends?

questions.questionText: Look at the pictures and tell the story.

content.prompt: Look at these pictures and tell the story of the treasure map adventure.
followUpQuestions: Where did the children find the map?
What problem did they have at the cave?
How did they cross the river?
How did they feel at the end?
